name: avalonia-layout-zafiro
description: Guidelines for modern Avalonia UI layout using Zafiro.Avalonia, emphasizing shared styles, generic components, and avoiding XAML redundancy.
capabilities: Guide semantic container usage with HeaderedContainer, EdgePanel, and Card components
Apply shared styles and DynamicResource for consistent theming
Implement IconExtension markup for FontAwesome icons
Use Xaml.Interaction.Behaviors for UI logic without code-behind
Create generic reusable components to reduce XAML nesting
Organize theme resources across Colors, Styles, and App axaml files
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 100 pattern matches but all are false positives. The skill contains only Markdown documentation files with XAML examples for Avalonia UI development. Detected patterns like backticks are Markdown code fences, not shell execution. No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: Create maintainable Avalonia applications using semantic containers and shared styles. Reduce XAML redundancy with Zafiro.Avalonia components and best practices.
userTitle: Build clean Avalonia UI layouts with Zafiro
limitations: Documentation only - does not execute or generate actual XAML code
Focused on Zafiro.Avalonia library patterns, not raw Avalonia
Does not cover custom control development

useCases.title: Desktop App Developer
useCases.description: Refactor existing Avalonia views to use semantic containers and shared styles for cleaner, maintainable XAML.

useCases.targetUser: Technical leads designing Avalonia application architecture
useCases.title: UI Architect
useCases.description: Establish consistent UI patterns across a large Avalonia codebase using Zafiro components and theme organization.

useCases.targetUser: Developers new to Avalonia from WPF or other XAML frameworks
useCases.title: Cross-Platform Developer
useCases.description: Learn modern Avalonia layout techniques that work across Windows, macOS, and Linux platforms.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Container Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to create a settings section with a header and content. Which Zafiro container should I use and how?

promptTemplates.title: Icon Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to add FontAwesome icons to buttons and headers using IconExtension with consistent styling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding visual elements to UI components

promptTemplates.title: Style Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: I have repeated property settings across multiple HeaderedContainer elements. Help me extract these into a shared style using Classes.

promptTemplates.title: Behavior Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to handle focus management for a TextBox without using code-behind. Show me how to implement this with Interaction.Behaviors.

bestPractices: Use DynamicResource for colors and brushes to support theme switching
Flatten layouts with EdgePanel instead of nested Grid and StackPanel structures
Extract repeated layouts into generic UserControl components for reusability
antiPatterns: Using hardcoded colors or sizes directly in view XAML instead of resources
Creating deep nesting with multiple Grid and StackPanel layers
Using IValueConverter for simple logic that belongs in the ViewModel

faq.question: What is Zafiro.Avalonia?
faq.answer: Zafiro.Avalonia is a library providing enhanced UI components for Avalonia, including semantic containers like HeaderedContainer and EdgePanel, plus icon management via IconExtension.

faq.question: How do I add Zafiro.Avalonia to my project?
faq.answer: Install the Zafiro.Avalonia NuGet package. Reference the namespace in your XAML with xmlns:zafiro='using:Zafiro.Avalonia' or similar based on your project structure.

faq.question: When should I use Behaviors instead of code-behind?
faq.answer: Use Interaction.Behaviors for reusable UI logic like focus management or animations. This keeps XAML clean and allows independent testing of UI behaviors.

faq.question: What is the benefit of semantic containers?
faq.answer: Semantic containers like HeaderedContainer express intent clearly, reduce XAML verbosity, and provide built-in styling hooks via Classes for consistent theming.

faq.question: How do I switch between light and dark themes?
faq.answer: Define colors and brushes as DynamicResource in Colors.axaml. Reference them in styles. Swap resource dictionaries at runtime to switch themes without rebuilding views.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with existing Avalonia projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidelines apply to any Avalonia project. You can incrementally adopt Zafiro components and shared style patterns as you refactor existing views.
